Some Lebanese Christian villages ‘asked to be annexed’ by Israel, Netanyahu says
items.summary:
Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said on Sunday that some Christian villages in southern Lebanon had asked to be annexed by Israel so that they could be protected from Hezbollah militants.
Lebanon was drawn into the wider war in the Middle East on March 2 when Hezbollah fired rockets at Israel in retaliation for the killing of Iran’s supreme leader in US-Israeli strikes.
Israel responded

items.title: Can Iran Keep Its Edge?
items.summary: Closing the Strait of Hormuz was a huge source of leverage during the war. But playing the same card over and over is risky.

items.title: With a Fragile Ceasefire under Threat, What Future for the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran prompted Tehran to close the Strait of Hormuz, allowing only selected ships to pass through. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Yasmine Farouk, Chris Newton and Ali Vaez consider what could come next in this vital maritime trade corridor.

items.title: The Funeral of Iran’s Supreme Leader, Ali Khamenei
items.summary: Iran’s new leaders are commemorating Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed on the first day of the U.S.-Israeli war against the country, with a funeral unlike any other in Iran’s recent history.

items.title: Exhausted by Iran War, Tehran Transforms for Khamenei’s Funeral
items.summary: Throughout the capital, the government is using Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s funeral to project strength at a time of great uncertainty for Iran.

items.title: CATL Invests in New Zealand Firm to Develop Graphite From Wood
items.summary: Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd. is investing in a New Zealand-headquartered company that converts forestry byproducts into graphite for use in lithium batteries.

Oil Edges Lower on OPEC Increase, Dollar Steady: Markets Wrap
items.summary: Oil opened lower as energy flows through the Strait of Hormuz persisted and OPEC+ signaled higher supplies. Traders were also focused on the Korean won’s first day of 24-hour trading.

items.title: Oil Drops as Flows in Hormuz Persist and OPEC+ Flags More Supply
items.summary: Oil fell as energy flows through the Strait of Hormuz persisted and OPEC+ signaled higher supplies, fanning concerns about a potential glut.

Australia Built a Gas Export Empire. Now the Backlash Is Here
items.summary: Australia’s liquefied natural gas exporters should be riding high on a A$20 billion ($14 billion) sales windfall from the conflict in the Middle East. Instead, their good fortune has triggered a wave of public backlash.

items.title: Hardcore History’s DanCarlin Explains: Where Do We Go From Here?
items.summary:
This Independence Day marks the 250th anniversary of America. But with an increasing amount of polarization and conflict, is this holiday able to bring the country together?

Dan Carlin, host of Hardcore History, joins Max Chafkin on this bonus episode of the Everybody's Business podcast to answer the question, “Have we been through this before?” (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: EU and NATO Members Grapple with War in Iran
items.summary: This week on War & Peace, Olga speaks with Crisis Group’s head of EU Affairs, Lisa Musiol, about how the EU, its members and European members of NATO have responded to the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran and what it reveals about EU foreign policy and its constraints.
